Private Thought of Tanaka: Huh, Ito-san just messaged. He wants "out-of-the-box" thinking, and she's feeling like she needs to lean into that "free ideas" directive, even though her nature leans more towards structure.

Maybe I can frame it in a way that allows us to explore truly out-there ideas without completely abandoning the momentum I have with Watanabe-san. Perhaps some of these "wild" ideas could even become interesting features or alternate modes within a broader "Chronoscape Curator" universe, or perhaps even inspire a completely separate, equally compelling idea if we hit on something truly brilliant. We still have six turns, so there's a little room to explore.

I should talk to Ito-san next. I need to acknowledge her request and the importance of Takahashi-san's feedback. I can offer to brainstorm, but maybe I can also gently steer the conversation to see if her "wild" ideas could somehow intersect with the kind of unique, contemplative approach we're already exploring, rather than just starting from scratch. Or maybe we just need a dedicated "wild ideas" session that's separate from current project focus, just to get it all out. It's important to keep all options open for now. After that, I'll need to reconnect with Watanabe-san to either loop her into this new brainstorming, or confirm our next steps on "Chronoscape Curator" before the ideas get too scattered. (formed immediately after interacting with Ito)
